description: A query or index change was justified by EXPLAIN output from empty or unrealistic fixture data.
body: Failure mode: a plan looks cheap in tests but scans or sorts too much real data because fixture cardinality and parameter distribution are unrealistic. Diagnostic trigger: plan evidence lacks row counts, representative predicates, or before/after comparison. Do-not-use signal: do not accept EXPLAIN proof without data-scale context for performance-sensitive queries. Recovery: build representative synthetic or anonymized data, capture before/after plans with common and worst-case predicates, and check write-path impact of new indexes. Risk boundary: if realistic data cannot be used, report limited confidence instead of presenting the index as proven.
